State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


May 2, 1983


Arthur Griffin, M.D.
Westside Hospital District
109 Adkisson Way
Taft, CA 93268

                                                Re: Your Request for Advice, Our
                                                    File No. A-83-102

Dear Dr. Griffin:

    Your letter raises a series of questions regarding the
publication of an informational brochure/ prior to the last
election of the Board of Directors of the West Side Hospital
District. Because your letter questions the past actions of
other officials, we are unable to advise you with regard to
those actions. However, we have referred your letter to our
Enforcement Division for their review.

    With respect to your questions regarding decisions on
possible future publication of somewhat similar brochures, we
can only provide you with general advice at this time. If you
are faced with a specific decision in the future, please write
to us at that time and we will be happy to provide you with
specific advice.

    Generally, a public official may not make, participate in
making, or use his or her official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he or she has a financial
interest. Government Code Section 87100. Thus, if publication
of a brochure would have a material financial effect on a
financial interest,2/ the decision to publish it might be the
basis for a conflict of interest. However, there first must be
a financial interest present. As discussed thorougly in my


_________________________

1/  This brochure contained reports on the progress made
    by the hospital district and had photographs of the former
    President of the Board, who was running for re-election, and the
    hospital administrator.

2/  Government Code Section 87103.

Arthur Griffin, M.D.
May 2, 1983
Page Two


earlier letter to you, the District is not a source of income to
you. Section 82030(b)(1). You have no investment or real
property which would be affected by the decision.3/ Nor will
any business entity of which you are an officer or employee be
affected by the decision to publish the brochure. Consequently,
there is no financial interest to be affected and, therefore, no
basis for a conflict of interest requiring disqualification.

We note, however, that if the brochure specifically
advocated the candidacy of certain individuals, then its
production and distribution at public expense might well
constitute a reportable contribution or expenditure under the
Political Reform Act or violate other provisions of the
statutory or common law. You may wish to consult with Roy
Gargano regarding these issues.

Should you have further questions, please do not hesitate to
contact me at 916/322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leigh
Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km
CC: Roy Gargano, District Counsel




3/ Id.

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901        322-6441           322-6444


April 20, 1983


Arthur Griffin, M.D.
109 Adkisson Way
Taft, CA 93268

Re: A-83-102

Dear Dr. Griffin:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief, Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

FPPC

APR 12 8 56 AM '03                                                April 7, 1982


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804

Gentlemen:

I am a member of the Board of Directors of West Side Hospital District
and was elected to office in November, 1982. I herewith wish to express my
appreciation to you for previously clarifying certain matters regarding a
possible conflict of interest.

After studying the information that you sent to me regarding "the
Act", I wish to ask your opinion regarding another matter and submit the
following facts and supporting evidence:

1. A brochure entitled "West Side District Hospital - A Progress
Report, 1980-1982" praising the West Side Hospital District was
secretly prepared and placed as an insert in two local newspapers
approximately FIVE DAYS BEFORE THE ELECTION of the West Side Hospital
District Board of Directors in November, 1982. The information in
the brochure was presented in such a manner as to indicate that the
Hospital District was in excellent financial condition and that the
Hospital was performing an outstanding service and did not mention
the huge financial deficits generated and total lack of improvement
in patient census. The brochure contained a picture of the Chairman
of the Board of Directors of the Hospital District. This Board
Chairman, whose name is David March, was running for re-election at
the time that the brochure was prepared and published.

2. This brochure was published as an insert in The Daily Midway
Drillers and in The Bakersfield Californian, which are daily newspapers
distributed in the West Side Hospital District. In addition, the
brochure was prepared by and at the expense of the Hospital District.
The cost of the preparation and printing of the brochure was in excess
of $3,200.00.

3. The Hospital District has never before published such a "progress
report" in the form of a paid advertisement and has never before prepared such a brochure for widespread public distribution.

Based on the above facts, please submit to me the answers to the
following questions:

1. In your opinion, does this publication constitute political advertising and personal gain for the public officials (West Side Hospital
District Board Member) who were running for re-election?

- 1 -

2. If your answer to the above question is negative, would a similar publication by the Hospital District containing my picture and glowing reports of progress and accomplishments of the Hospital during my tenure in office, to be published approximately one week before the next election when I will be a candidate for re-election, be free of a conflict of interest?

3. Could I be subject to prosecution for violations of "the Act" if I participated in a decision by the Board of Directors to pay for such a publication?

A copy of the brochure referred to above is enclosed, along with a copy of a paid political advertisement in response to the published brochure.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Arthur Griffin, M.D.

AGrif
Enc. 2

sections.question_synthetic: Dr. Griffin sought advice regarding whether publication of an informational brochure by the Westside Hospital District prior to a board election would create a conflict of interest, and whether similar future publications would raise conflict of interest concerns.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded there is no conflict of interest basis for disqualification regarding the brochure publication because Dr. Griffin has no financial interest that would be affected by the decision. However, if the brochure advocated for specific candidates, it could constitute a reportable contribution/expenditure under the Political Reform Act or violate other statutory provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers found; extracted from body text. Second page appears partially degraded/scanned poorly but provides supporting regulatory context. File reference A-83-102 confirms this is an advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Dr. Griffin sought advice regarding whether publication of an informational brochure by the Westside Hospital District prior to a board election would create a conflict of interest, and whether similar future publications would raise conflict of interest concerns.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC concluded there is no conflict of interest basis for disqualification regarding the brochure publication because Dr. Griffin has no financial interest that would be affected by the decision. However, if the brochure advocated for specific candidates, it could constitute a reportable contribution/expenditure under the Political Reform Act or violate other statutory provisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised Dr. Griffin regarding a hospital district brochure published before a board election, concluding no conflict of interest exists because the district is not a source of his income and he has no affected financial interests, though cautioning that candidate advocacy in the brochure could raise other legal issues.
